description: Shifts a 3D vector backward (opposite to its direction) by a scaled multiple of its length. The vector is translated in the reverse direction.
utility: Shift a 3D vector backward along its direction by a scaled distance.

arguments.name: scalar
arguments.description: Scale factor for the shift distance. Defaults to 1 (shift by the vector's own length)

alternates.description: Shift backward by the vector's own length
Shift backward by scalar times the vector's length

examples.scenario: Shift vector backward by its own length
examples.expression: G = g3d(at(4,20), view(20, 20)); V = vector3d(G, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0); V2 = backward3d(V)

examples.scenario: Shift vector backward by half its length
Shift vector backward by twice its length
examples.expression: G = g3d(at(4,20), view(20, 20)); V = vector3d(G, 1, 1, 1, 3, 1, 1); V2 = backward3d(V, 2)
